name: Carriage 5 — The Dining Car
summary: A dining car laid for a meal that was almost served, then put on hold for longer than meals can be put on hold.

notable_features: Tables set for four. The napkins are folded in a way the Train no longer remembers being folded. The cutlery is laid for a second course already, before anyone has eaten the first.

On the nearest table a wine glass holds a half-finger of red. The meniscus is still wet against the rim. The chair is pushed back at an angle. No one has been here in a long time and no one has just left.
The menu has dates printed in the corner. The day matches today. The year matches one you cannot place. The script is a typeface no printer has cut in living memory.
A folded napkin on the centre table is heavier than a napkin. There is a number written on it in pencil, in a hand that is steady in three of its digits and not steady in one.
The kitchen door at the far end is brushed steel, with a three-wheel combination latch. The wheels are warm to the touch, in the way metal that has just been turned is warm.

san_triggers.trigger_text: The wine has not skinned over. No one has been at the table for a long time.
The date on the menu is today. The year on the menu is not now.
The chandeliers flicker not at random. They flicker on the off-beat of the wheels.

skill_check_hooks.action_hint: examine the place settings for the missing piece
study the pencil note on the napkin
listen at the kitchen door before opening it

puzzle.description: Three digits unlock the kitchen door. Each digit lives on a different surface in the room.
puzzle.unlock_condition: Player assembles the 3-digit combination from a Spot Hidden (regular) on the table settings, a Library Use (regular) on the menu, and a Spot Hidden (hard) on the napkin note — OR a Mechanical Repair (hard) success forces the latch.
